mechanism_narrative: Strong monthly job growth (PAYEMS) signals robust labor market conditions that boost consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 60% of periods when PAYEMS growth exceeds 150k units.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) below 4.0% signals accommodative monetary policy that lowers discount rates and stimulates equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX shows negative 5d returns in 75%+ of instances where FEDFUNDS declines below 4.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) indicates strong consumer demand that drives corporate revenue growth, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX fails to generate positive 60d returns when both RSAFS and HOUST show >2% monthly growth.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in industrial production (INDPRO) above 105.0 signals expanding supply capacity that reduces inflationary pressures and supports equity valuations, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX shows negative 20d returns when INDPRO exceeds 105.0 in 60%+ of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent monthly inflation (CPIAUCSL) above 3.0% triggers expectations of rate hikes that increase equity discount rates, causing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 50% of periods when CPIAUCSL exceeds 3.0%.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly decline in GDP (GDPC1) combined with rising unemployment (UNRATE) indicates weakening demand conditions that depress corporate earnings, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX shows positive 60d returns when both GDPC1 declines and UNRATE rises by >0.5pp.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly drop in unemployment (UNRATE) below 3.5% signals tight labor market conditions that drive wage inflation and corporate cost pressures, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in 70%+ of instances when UNRATE falls below 3.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly contraction in industrial production (INDPRO) below 104.0 combined with rising inflation (CPIAUCSL) indicates supply-side shocks that increase corporate costs and reduce earnings, causing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX shows positive 20d returns when INDPRO < 104.0 and CPIAUCSL > 3.0% in 50%+ of cases.
